name: Inventory Turnover Risk Calculator
description: Industrial-grade risk assessment for inventory turnover performance, incorporating variability, obsolescence, and carrying cost exposure. Aligns with ISO 9001, Lean, Six Sigma, and WERC DC Metrics.

inputs.label: Average Inventory Value (USD)
inputs.businessContext: Total average value of inventory over the period (e.g., monthly average of COGS-based inventory).

businessContext_i18n.en: Total average value of inventory over the period (e.g., monthly average of COGS-based inventory).

inputs.label: Annual Cost of Goods Sold (USD)
inputs.businessContext: Total cost of goods sold over the last 12 months.

businessContext_i18n.en: Total cost of goods sold over the last 12 months.

inputs.label: Demand Variability Coefficient (CV)
inputs.businessContext: Coefficient of variation of monthly demand (standard deviation / mean). Higher values indicate greater unpredictability.

businessContext_i18n.en: Coefficient of variation of monthly demand (standard deviation / mean). Higher values indicate greater unpredictability.

inputs.label: Obsolescence Rate (% per year)
inputs.businessContext: Annual percentage of inventory value that becomes obsolete or expired.

businessContext_i18n.en: Annual percentage of inventory value that becomes obsolete or expired.

inputs.label: Inventory Carrying Cost Rate (% per year)
inputs.businessContext: Annual cost to hold inventory (storage, insurance, capital, etc.) as a percentage of inventory value.

label_i18n.en: Inventory Carrying Cost Rate (% per year)

businessContext_i18n.en: Annual cost to hold inventory (storage, insurance, capital, etc.) as a percentage of inventory value.

inputs.label: Average Lead Time (Days)
inputs.businessContext: Average replenishment lead time from order placement to receipt.

businessContext_i18n.en: Average replenishment lead time from order placement to receipt.

inputs.label: Service Level Target (%)
inputs.businessContext: Target fill rate or cycle service level (e.g., 95% = 0.95).

businessContext_i18n.en: Target fill rate or cycle service level (e.g., 95% = 0.95).

inputs.label: Inventory Record Accuracy (%)
inputs.businessContext: Percentage of inventory records matching physical counts (cycle count accuracy).

businessContext_i18n.en: Percentage of inventory records matching physical counts (cycle count accuracy).

inputs.label: Industry Benchmark Turnover (turns/year)
inputs.businessContext: Expected turnover rate for the industry (e.g., WERC benchmark).

businessContext_i18n.en: Expected turnover rate for the industry (e.g., WERC benchmark).

rules.message: Average inventory value must be greater than zero.
Annual COGS must be greater than zero.
Demand variability coefficient must be between 0 and 2.
Obsolescence rate must be between 0 and 100.
Carrying cost rate must be between 0 and 100.
Lead time must be greater than 0 days.
Service level target must be between 50% and 99.99%.
Inventory accuracy must be between 0% and 100%.
Industry benchmark turnover must be greater than zero.

thresholds.message: Turnover is within 80% of industry benchmark. Low risk.
Turnover is between 50% and 80% of benchmark. Moderate risk.
Turnover is below 50% of industry benchmark. High risk of excess inventory and obsolescence.
Carrying cost rate exceeds 30%. Consider reducing inventory levels.
Obsolescence rate exceeds 10%. Immediate review of slow-moving items required.
Inventory record accuracy below 95%. Risk of stockouts and excess safety stock.

formulas.name: Basic Inventory Turnover
formulas.description: Standard turnover ratio: COGS / Average Inventory.

formulas.name: Adjusted Turnover (Demand Variability)
formulas.description: Turnover penalized by demand variability using a Six Sigma approach (higher CV reduces effective turns).

formulas.name: Obsolescence Impact Factor
formulas.description: Factor representing the drag on turnover due to obsolescence.

formulas.name: Carrying Cost Exposure
formulas.description: Annual carrying cost as a percentage of inventory value.

formulas.name: Safety Stock Factor (Lead Time & Service Level)
formulas.description: Z-score based on service level (normal approximation) times lead time variability. Uses standard z for service level.

formulas.name: Inventory Accuracy Adjustment
formulas.description: Penalty factor for record inaccuracy (Lean principle: accuracy drives waste).

formulas.name: Inventory Turnover Risk Score (Effective Turns)
formulas.description: Primary result: risk-adjusted turnover considering variability, obsolescence, safety stock, and accuracy. Lower values indicate higher risk.

primary.label: Effective Inventory Turnover (Risk-Adjusted)
primary.description: Primary risk-adjusted turnover metric. Lower than basic turnover indicates higher risk from variability, obsolescence, or inaccuracy.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Demand Variability Loss
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Lost turnover potential due to demand unpredictability.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Inventory Inaccuracy Loss
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Imputed loss from record inaccuracy (waste, expediting, stockouts).

suggestedActions.action: Implement demand sensing and S&OP to reduce variability. Consider postponement strategies.
Conduct ABC-XYZ analysis and phase out slow movers. Use markdown optimization.
Implement cycle counting program (ISO 9001:2015 clause 7.1.5). Use barcode/RFID.
Review safety stock levels using Six Sigma DMAIC. Reduce lead time variability.
Negotiate lower storage rates, reduce excess inventory, or use consignment.

dataConfidenceAdjusted.label: Data Confidence Score
dataConfidenceAdjusted.description: Aggregate confidence in input data quality (0-1). Based on accuracy and completeness of inputs.
